raw_consumer_statement:
In XXXX I applied for a XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX because I intended to travel more on this cruiseline. I never received a response from the associated bank with this card. 

In XX/XX/XXXX I received formal letter from Barclays bank asking me if I still wanted this credit card ( that I appied for in XXXX )? If so, please call them which I did and they asked me to temporarily unfreeze my credit so they could verify the information they were seeking. I did that and while I was on the phone with Barclay representative I was told my card had been approved and it would be coming to me. It showed up in the mail some days later. I activated the card and had it on my desk to use for an upcoming cruise In XXXX, XXXX. 

I have several letters to support what I write below. On XX/XX/XXXX an unauthorized {$1000.00} balance transfer was initiated by Barclay from a long-held XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX ) credit card of mine. {$1000.00} flowed to the Barclay Visa and Barclay also charged me a {$50.00} fee. 
I DID NOT AUTHORIZE THIS transfer and don't know how it happened.

A few days later I checked my credit cards and was surprised to see the transfer to the Barclay Visa. I contacted Barclay was immediately transferred to the Fraud department. I was told it was suspicious that a credit card I applied for in XXXX had triggered a letter to me in XXXX. The same day they shut down the account number and ordered a new card number and reissue of the card. Barclay agreed to return the {$1000.00} to XXXX. They documented all of this in a letter to me dated XX/XX/XXXX. 

In the mean time I contacted XXXX and according the representative I spoke with they could not validate how the request for a bank transfer occurred. In a letter they sent to me on XX/XX/XXXX it appears as if the transfer then the return of the funds to the XXXX account happened all on the same day, XX/XX/XXXX. 

My complaint is : {$1000.00} was returned to my XXXX  account minus the balance I had for the XXXX Visa leaving me a balance of {$0.00}. 

Barclay 's bank agreed in early XXXX to return the {$1000.00} as well as the {$50.00} balance transfer fee they charged me. I spent quite a bit of time talking with the Fraud area of Barclays. 

Later in XXXX, BARCLAY 's REVERSED their decision and charged my account with a {$1000.00} that they want me to pay. I DON'T WANT TO PAY for SOMETHING I DIDN'T AUTHORIZE. 

I have some paperwork from both banks and I find it to be very ambiguous. 

I have letters from both banks and look forward to clarifying this issue. Regards, XXXX XXXX
